Recent News

  • Feb. 2026 Submitted two papers to CITA 2026: HERMES (Knowledge Graph Reasoning for Patient Outcome Prediction) and Random Undersampling for Intrusion Detection.
  • Jan. 2026 Three journal papers submitted: STGraph-FS (IEEE TAI), LLM-Powered UAV IDS (Cluster Computing), and PIKE (IEEE TNNLS).
  • Dec. 2025 Our paper on Training-Free MoA for Multi-Document Summarization was revised and resubmitted to Neural Computing and Applications.
  • Sep. 2025 Our paper on Training-Free MoA for MDS was submitted to Neural Computing and Applications.
  • Aug. 2025 Started as Research Assistant at Business AI Lab, NEU.

I am a Research Assistant at Applied AI Lab (Phenikaa University), where I am supervised by Dr. Trang Mai Xuan. I also work closely with Dr. Thien Van Luong at Business AI Lab (National Economics University). My research primarily focuses on AI for Healthcare.

I am actively seeking PhD positions in Computer Science, with a focus on Multi-Agent Systems, LLMs, and AI for Healthcare. If you have opportunities or would like to collaborate, please feel free to reach out!

Generative AI & Multimodal Learning

Modeling across text–image–signal–structured data; multimodal fusion and VLMs; predictive representation learning (JEPA-style) for robust, compact, and transferable features.

Agentic AI & Multi-Agent Systems

Designing LLM-driven multi-agent frameworks (coordination, tool-use, planning, self-reflection) to collaboratively solve complex tasks such as multi-document summarization.

Efficient & Trustworthy LLMs

RAG and graph-augmented RAG, quantization/distillation (e.g., QLoRA/AWQ), inference optimization (KV-cache, batching), privacy/security for self-hosted deployments.

Applied AI

Healthcare (clinical NLP, EHR/text–image fusion, decision support) and Business (analytics, knowledge management, operations) with measurable impact.
